230054.explanation: Stadsbelang highly values participation. We govern not for the residents but with the residents. A citizen's council is a tool that can be effectively used to amplify residents' voices.

230055.explanation: Stadsbelang is an advocate of 'the neighborhood governs'; to strengthen this, neighborhood councils should also receive budgets.

230056.explanation: Gorinchem already has multiple bodies where people with complaints and problems can turn to (for example, the disputes committee). Adding an ombudsman to existing bodies is unnecessary spending that could be better used for the resident.

230059.explanation: Property tax originally applies to real estate. Entrepreneurs who rent premises do not have to contribute to it, encouraging new entrepreneurs to settle in attractive locations without immediately incurring high (extra) costs.

230060.explanation: Gorinchem is a beautiful, well-maintained city that incurs various costs. Tourists visiting Gorinchem can contribute to the city's maintenance through the tourist tax.

230061.explanation: Stadsbelang believes there must be a good balance between living and recreation. This is currently the case, allowing us to earn good income from tourism while it remains pleasant to live here. Tourism income should not come at the expense of residents. Spending extra to attract tourists could disrupt this balance and harm residents’ living comfort and livability.

230062.explanation: Stadsbelang believes we should continue investing in the city. It is important that we can continue to bear our expenses, but accelerated debt reduction is not necessary.

230063.explanation: Stadsbelang wants to use the existing docks for cargo ships outside the Waterpoort multifunctionally so that we do not have to invest but can generate extra income.

230064.explanation: Temporary use does not contribute to a structurally lively shopping area. Stadsbelang therefore believes scarce public funds should not be used commercially.

230065.explanation: Stadsbelang believes rules regarding advertising should be regulated nationally and are not the responsibility of local government.

230067.explanation: Stadsbelang believes existing shelter is in line with current obligations. This number is also what Gorinchem can accommodate in terms of provision levels.

230068.explanation: Stadsbelang believes existing shelter is in line with current obligations. This number is also what Gorinchem can accommodate in terms of provision levels.

230069.explanation: The Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers (COA) is responsible for refugees in Gorinchem as well as their language lessons and integration.

230071.explanation: Gorinchem has sufficient places where dogs can run off-leash. Expanding these locations limits options for residents who experience this as unpleasant or unsafe.

230072.explanation: Stadsbelang believes that plants and trees should be encouraged, but making it mandatory goes too far. Residents should retain freedom of choice over their own properties.

230073.explanation: Stadsbelang believes that the city center is not a suitable location for such a large commercial party/festival. The many road closures and deployment of emergency services make the city inaccessible and incur high costs for the municipality.

230074.explanation: Stadsbelang advocates for an accessible bulky waste policy to prevent illegal dumping and adding extra waste. Monthly bulky waste collection without additional costs contributes to this.

230075.explanation: As long as solar panels do not damage the building, alter the street view, or are visible from the street, solar panels should be allowed on monuments.

230076.explanation: Stadsbelang remains critical of wind turbines. They insist that the 500-meter distance from housing remains a firm condition and prefer to look for alternatives.

230077.explanation: The school breakfast will have a major impact on the start or lesson times of primary schools. Stadsbelang would, however, like to see tailored solutions where needed, as well as school fruit or a school lunch implemented.

230078.explanation: Holding real estate is not the primary task of the municipality. Properties can be sold when they no longer serve a social purpose. It is important, however, that the municipality includes clear conditions with the sale to ensure the appearance is preserved.

230079.explanation: Artificial turf has lower maintenance costs, reducing expenses for clubs and preventing these costs from being passed on to the residents who use the facilities.

230080.explanation: Stadsbelang is a strong advocate for the Culture House (library and theater in one building). This supports the cultural sector, cross-pollination of disciplines, society, and cohesion. Combining the library and theater also saves the costs of maintaining two separate buildings.

230081.explanation: Art in public spaces contributes to a pleasant and healthy living environment. It promotes social cohesion and access to meeting places.

230082.explanation: This is not a government task, but an educational one, and requires tailored approaches that schools understand better than the municipality.

230084.explanation: Stadsbelang is not against sex work, but believes it must be well regulated. Allowing sex work in private homes raises concerns about poor working conditions and nuisance for neighbors.

230085.explanation: Stadsbelang wants to take a tough approach to illegal dumping because it burdens residents who behave properly.

230086.explanation: Entertainment venues have their own cameras. Stadsbelang supports having hosts/hostesses (sus-teams, neighborhood stewards, etc.) present during evenings and nights to act preventively.

230089.explanation: Stadsbelang believes existing plans should be executed before making new ones. No stacking of plans, but execution.

230090.explanation: In recent years many single-family homes have been built in Gorinchem, but housing shortage persists, especially making it difficult for starters to enter the market. Prioritizing high-rise buildings can quickly add many homes.

230091.explanation: Vacant buildings can and should be converted into homes. However, granting subsidies is unnecessary spending; changing the zoning plan already benefits the property owner through increased property value.

230093.explanation: To realize more homes and desirable mobility, the right more homes in the cheap and affordable segments need to be built.

230095.explanation: Stadsbelang supports alternative forms of housing, including flexible housing. It is important, however, that costs are always weighed against benefits.

230096.explanation: Stadsbelang stands for all residents of Gorinchem and does not want to introduce a negatively perceived card, but a Gorinchem pass that everyone could have. Stadsbelang supports possible benefits this could offer to low-income individuals.

230097.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits should contribute to the Gorinchem community by performing socially meaningful work if they are able to do so.

230100.explanation: Parking pressure in the city center of Gorinchem is already very high. Reducing the number of parking spaces will further increase this pressure and negatively affect the accessibility of the city.

230102.explanation: The number of charging points for electric cars should be demand-driven. Installing charging points is not a goal in itself.

230103.explanation: Stadsbelang believes Riveer should take a facilitating role in establishing a ferry connection while the Merwedebrug is under maintenance, but the payment should be the responsibility of Rijkswaterstaat and not the municipality.

230104.explanation: For the safety of cyclists and other road users, the maximum speed should be lowered where there is no bicycle path.

230105.explanation: The widening of the A15 contributes to better traffic flow and less cut-through traffic through Gorinchem, which improves accessibility and the living environment in Gorinchem.

230106.explanation: The Kweeklust parking garage is currently not used to its full capacity. Expanding Kweeklust is therefore an unnecessary expenditure at this time.

230107.explanation: A dedicated bicycle street causes unclear traffic situations elsewhere in the city, which lowers safety instead of increasing it.

230108.explanation: For the city's attractiveness, Stadsbelang would like to see free or reduced-rate parking on Sundays.

230109.explanation: Stadsbelang would like to see the Groenmarkt become truly green and attractive again. The parking pressure will not increase because the Kweeklust parking garage is not used to its maximum capacity.

230112.explanation: The municipality cannot bear or take over all responsibilities. Social funds must be used as responsibly and as broadly supported as possible.

230113.explanation: The municipality cannot bear or take over all responsibilities. Social funds must be used as responsibly and as broadly supported as possible. Support for low-income groups via, for example, the food bank, already exists and must continue.

230114.explanation: A general practitioner in every neighborhood is a necessity. If municipal support is needed for this, Stadsbelang supports it.

230115.explanation: Stadsbelang focuses on community centers, not hangout spots. Hangout spots cause neighborhood nuisance, whereas community centers reduce nuisance.

230116.explanation: According to Stadsbelang, the main hall of the Nieuwe Doelen should become part of the Culture House. The Culture House focuses on amateur art, but also allows subsidized artists to use it.

230054.explanation: We focus on neighborhood-oriented work and neighborhood budgets.

230055.explanation: More space for personal choices within the neighborhood.

230060.explanation: An extra euro from tourists helps keep the city beautiful.

230061.explanation: Let's leave it to the market. The municipality already does what is necessary.

230062.explanation: New construction of homes and schools takes priority as long as debts remain manageable.

230065.explanation: Young people are too vulnerable to excessive gambling advertising.

230067.explanation: Gorinchem already does enough in terms of shelter. We believe neighboring municipalities should take their turn. A municipality should not provide more shelter than it can handle. We already have waiting lists for housing and youth care.

230068.explanation: At the moment, there is no room because we already do a lot for shelter. Consider the housing shortage and waiting lists for youth care. Neighboring municipalities need to take action.

230069.explanation: That is already sufficiently arranged. No extra funding is needed.

230071.explanation: Yes, in some neighborhoods we lack places.

230072.explanation: That is not the municipality's responsibility. Encouraging it is fine, but making it mandatory is absurd.

230073.explanation: Summer festivals belong in the city center. They should remain manageable and enjoyable for residents and visitors.

230074.explanation: Definitely, that is a key point for Democrats Gorinchem.

230075.explanation: Acceptable as long as the historic appearance remains intact.

230076.explanation: There are no other suitable locations in Gorinchem.

230077.explanation: We should look at who really needs it.

230078.explanation: It is possible but under strict conditions.

230079.explanation: It lasts longer and requires much less maintenance. Sports activities can continue regardless of the weather.

230080.explanation: Use is only possible if the municipality contributes to the costs.

230081.explanation: The money is currently needed for housing.

230082.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

230084.explanation: Sex work in homes leads to deterioration and insecurity in the neighborhood.

230085.explanation: Dumping of waste must be tackled firmly.

230086.explanation: Yes, because there is currently too much violence and nuisance.

230089.explanation: We must seize every opportunity for more housing.

230090.explanation: Offers more opportunities for first-time buyers and the elderly. That is needed now.

230091.explanation: A good opportunity for more starter homes.

230093.explanation: That is sufficient for a balanced supply.

230095.explanation: Waiting times are currently too long. Flexible housing is a priority for Democrats Gorinchem.

230096.explanation: Being able to participate contributes to a better life and prevents costs in other areas.

230097.explanation: If possible, a reciprocal obligation is good because it contributes to the person's network and sense of purpose.

230100.explanation: Parking garages on the edges of the fortress should actually be expanded.

230102.explanation: The number of electric cars is increasing, and charging stations can be expanded proportionally.

230103.explanation: Too expensive for a limited solution.

230104.explanation: In general, we consider this a good measure for safety.

230105.explanation: It would greatly help Gorinchem if the A15 flows better. Just like a complete approach to Banneweg!

230106.explanation: Yes, that contributes to parking on the edges of the fortress and offers opportunities for an attractive city center.

230107.explanation: There are streets where cyclists deserve priority.

230108.explanation: A good boost for a lively city center on Sundays.

230109.explanation: That can only happen after the garages have been significantly expanded. Afterwards, Groenmarkt can be attractively arranged.

230112.explanation: That goes too far for a municipality that itself does not receive money for it from the national government.

230113.explanation: It is good to be able to collect it, but it goes too far for the municipality to actively distribute it.

230114.explanation: We want to prevent waiting lists for general practitioners. If necessary, by giving a push to a GP who wants to settle.

230115.explanation: If you don't offer something, young people will choose a place themselves. Therefore, we prefer to collaborate in finding good spots and want to make use of neighborhood centers.

230116.explanation: The cultural center will not be suitable for professional performances in the future. We prefer to spend the money on amateur groups and associations.

230054.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants to involve Gorcumers more in decisions. Also, Gorcumers who do not often make their voices heard can think along and advise through citizens' councils to make choices that are good for Gorinchem. This way, we want to reduce the gap between residents and politics.

230055.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes in the strength of our neighborhoods and the Gorcumers who live there. Residents know better than anyone what is going on and what is needed. Therefore, we want neighborhood councils with their own budgets so they can truly make a difference in their area.

230056.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes that Gorcumers should be helped quickly and fairly with problems involving the municipality. Therefore, we want an independent ombudsman who responds promptly, takes action, and supports residents so that everyone feels heard.

230059.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes that the property tax (OZB) for businesses and shops should rest with the owner. This keeps the tax fair and transparent, and prevents tenants, often small entrepreneurs, from being unnecessarily burdened.

230060.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants to increase the tourist tax. Visitors thus contribute to the maintenance of the city and to places that everyone uses. This way, Gorinchem remains pleasant to live in and visit.

230061.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes the municipality should not spend more money actively attracting visitors. Gorinchem already attracts many tourists as the most beautiful fortified city in the Netherlands. We prefer to spend money on facilities, schools, and greenery, so the city becomes better for Gorcumers.

230062.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes debts should be reduced where possible. But good school buildings for our children and traffic safety for all Gorcumers are indispensable. We do not want to cut back on these, because investing in education and safety is investing in Gorcumers.

230063.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem does not see a second berth for cruise ships as a priority. We want to invest in our Gorcumers and their living environment. But if companies want to contribute to this and it can be done responsibly, we are open to considering it.

230064.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants to be an attractive municipality for residents and visitors. We support initiatives in vacant buildings where this contributes to the living environment but do not automatically give subsidies to entrepreneurs; our priority is investing in Gorcumers and their facilities.

230065.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes advertisements for casinos should be banned. These ads are conspicuous and easily visible to young and vulnerable residents, making them more likely to be tempted to gamble and get into financial trouble. We want to prevent this.

230067.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem supports the dispersal law. We believe every municipality must take its responsibility. As a city, we accommodate the number of asylum seekers required by law. In emergencies, such as a new war, we are naturally ready to provide extra shelter.

230068.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes that minor asylum seekers (AMV's) must be accommodated as prescribed by law. We expect Gorcumers to obey the law, so we do as well. Preferably, we do this together with neighboring municipalities.

230069.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem finds language lessons and volunteer work important for proper participation in Gorinchem. Therefore, we want to invest more broadly so that not only refugees but also other groups such as low-literates receive good support.

230071.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes dogs should be able to play freely but always in safe and suitable locations. Well-defined off-leash areas protect nature and prevent nuisance to others. We do believe there should be more supervision to enforce the duty to clean up.

230072.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem thinks it is important to have more greenery in gardens but does not want to oblige residents to do so. However, we encourage planting trees and plants, for example through information, advice, and good examples.

230073.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem takes a neutral stance on summer festivals outside the center. It is up to the organizers, and if there is a need, we are happy to support with advice and finding a suitable location. We do not make it mandatory.

230074.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem finds it important that everyone can easily dispose of bulky waste, including those without a car or who for other reasons cannot go to the dump. Free collection once a month encourages reuse and helps prevent littering.

230075.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants to promote sustainability and energy savings, also in monuments. Solar panels are welcome provided they are placed carefully and fit with the appearance and heritage. This way, we combine the energy transition with preserving our historic buildings.

230076.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem finds sustainable energy important. There must always be careful consideration of a suitable location to ensure minimal nuisance to the environment. Clean energy is needed to reduce dependency on gas from countries like Russia.

230077.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes every child deserves a good start to the day. School breakfasts help increase equal opportunities. Where national schemes are insufficient, the municipality financially supports so that all children can benefit.

230078.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem finds it important to preserve historic buildings. Sales are possible but only under conditions that guarantee preservation, use, and accessibility, so the heritage remains for current and future generations.

230079.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem chooses natural grass for new sports fields. It is better for the environment and biodiversity, and safer for athletes. The KNVB increasingly advises natural grass over artificial turf, making it a sustainable and future-proof choice.

230080.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem sees many opportunities in combining the library and theater in one building. This creates a vibrant cultural center that is better utilized, more attractive to residents and visitors, and offers space for collaboration, education, and social activities.

230081.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants large construction projects to contribute to an attractive and livable public space. Art, but also greenery, plays an important role so that new places are inviting, sustainable, and valuable for everyone.

230082.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants every child to have equal opportunities to develop. Homework guidance helps and contributes to equal opportunities. This can be at school but also in other places such as community centers, close to children's living environment.

230084.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem opts for safety and realism. Sex work increasingly takes place online and from home. A ban pushes this out of sight and makes supervision impossible. We stand for protection of sex workers and clear, enforceable rules.

230085.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem chooses an approach that works. Camera surveillance probably causes the problem to shift elsewhere. We prefer to focus on better enforcement, clear rules, and targeted awareness so that illegal dumping is tackled structurally.

230086.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem sees camera surveillance in the nightlife area as a targeted measure to ensure safety. It helps with prevention and quick response to incidents, provided it is used carefully and with respect for the privacy of Gorcumers.

230089.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem sees Gorinchem-North as one of the few places for new housing. Here we can address the housing problem. We want to think in terms of solutions, so this must be carefully researched and planned so we can realize homes.

230090.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants sufficient homes for everyone. Because space is limited, we must make smart choices about housing types. Apartments are often logical, but we continue to support families. We consider per project what fits best with the environment and residents.

230091.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem deplores office vacancies while there is a large shortage of housing. With targeted support and subsidies, this can be a smart way to create homes within the limited space Gorinchem still has.

230093.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem finds it important that new construction remains affordable and feasible for everyone. Therefore, we want a cap on expensive rental or owner-occupied homes in new developments. This ensures plenty of affordable housing in Gorinchem, among others for starters and young people.

230095.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem sees flexible housing as a practical solution to ease the shortage in the short term. This way, young people and other starters get housing faster while working simultaneously on structural, sustainable housing market solutions.

230096.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem supports a discount pass for people with low incomes. This ensures everyone can play sports, follow a course, or attend a performance. Money should never be a reason not to participate. This keeps Gorinchem a city for everyone.

230097.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes people on social assistance should be helped to participate in society. Work or volunteer work can help, but must never be mandatory. We look at what someone can and wants and provide good guidance.

230100.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes fewer parking spaces are needed on-street within the fortress and wants more parking in garages. This creates more space for pedestrians and greenery. It ensures a pleasant and safe city center where people like to live and stay.

230102.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem promotes the use of electric cars by providing sufficient charging points. This makes clean transport easier, accelerates the switch to electric driving, and contributes to a better climate and cleaner air in the city.

230103.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes that a ferry during maintenance of the Merwede Bridge is not a municipal task. That is the responsibility of the province and the national government. Municipal funds are needed for local tasks such as housing, care, and safety.

230104.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes that cars should drive slower where there is no separate bike path. A maximum speed of 30 km/h makes the road safer for cyclists and pedestrians. This reduces the chance of accidents.

230105.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes that widening the A15 is not a good solution. More asphalt leads to more traffic and pollution. We want to focus on better public transport and safe alternatives so Gorinchem remains accessible and livable.

230106.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem thinks that expanding the Kweeklust parking garage can help park cars on the edge of the city. This keeps the city center more accessible and reduces traffic in the center.

230107.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes it is important that cyclists take priority in Gorinchem. More bicycle streets where they have priority over cars make traffic calmer and safer, reducing the chance of accidents.

230108.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes parking on Sundays does not have to be free. Parking fees help keep the city accessible and livable and provide funds for important facilities and maintenance.

230109.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants to make the Groenmarkt car-free, so the heart of the city becomes more attractive. Parking for disabled people will remain possible. This creates more space for people and places to stay in the center.

230112.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem believes support for families is important, but full reimbursement for relationship therapy goes too far. The municipality can provide support where needed but must make choices about how funds are spent.

230113.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem thinks it is important that the municipality offers free tampons and sanitary pads. Menstrual products are not a luxury. This ensures everyone can participate, even those with little money.

230114.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem wants good care for everyone to be nearby. General practitioners in every neighborhood provide quick access to medical help, better prevention, and continuity of care. Therefore, we financially support GPs who set up practices in neighborhoods without one.

230115.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem finds it important that young people have places in every neighborhood where they can safely meet. Well-designed hangout spots prevent nuisance, encourage social contacts and understanding between youth and residents, making the living environment more pleasant for everyone.

230116.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Gorinchem sees the Culture House primarily as a place to meet and organize activities together. We want to use the hall for local initiatives such as amateur arts and community groups. Extra funding for professional performances does not fit here.

230054.explanation: The VVD values participation and opts for involvement where engagement and responsibility are central, not for structural advising via randomly selected citizen councils.

230055.explanation: The VVD wants to actively involve residents in their neighborhoods and give space to resident initiatives with neighborhood budgets. Neighborhood councils can be a tool if they remain flexible and do not become mandatory or bureaucratic structures.

230056.explanation: Residents must be helped quickly and well. The VVD wants complaints to be resolved immediately without extra layers or counters; residents can also contact the National Ombudsman for this.

230059.explanation: The VVD wants predictable costs for entrepreneurs. Therefore, it opposes taxing users of business premises because these costs are often passed on through rent.

230060.explanation: An (even) higher tourist tax can deter visitors. Gorinchem must remain attractive for tourism and local spending.

230061.explanation: City marketing contributes to a lively city center and a strong economic profile. Actively promoting Gorinchem as a fortress city benefits tourism, hospitality, and entrepreneurs, strengthening the local economy.

230062.explanation: The VVD chooses financially healthy governance but wants to continue making necessary investments. Budget cuts on schools, roads, or safety to reduce debt are undesirable and harmful to Gorinchem's future.

230063.explanation: The VVD wants to strengthen city marketing and tourism focusing on domestic tourists, river cruises, and day-trippers.

230064.explanation: The VVD prefers to stimulate starters and temporary entrepreneurs through lower fees and practical support to reduce vacancy, not by giving money.

230065.explanation: The VVD believes advertising should be allowed as long as it complies with the law. Restrictions are only justified if there is demonstrable nuisance or safety risks, not based on ideological preferences.

230067.explanation: The VVD believes Gorinchem must take responsibility but no more than legally required. Due to limited space and pressure on facilities, it advocates sheltering within legal frameworks and a fair regional distribution.

230068.explanation: The VVD believes legal obligations must be met. Cooperation and possibilities with neighboring municipalities must be examined first. Balanced regional distribution to prevent overload of education and youth care is preferred.

230069.explanation: The VVD sees language proficiency as key to integration, work, and independence. Investing in language lessons and volunteer work helps newcomers participate faster and promotes mutual understanding in society.

230071.explanation: The VVD wants to allow recreation and pleasant coexistence in neighborhoods. Additional off-leash areas fit a livable public space, provided they are well integrated and do not cause nuisance to others.

230072.explanation: The VVD supports greening to combat heat stress and water nuisance but does not want to impose this as an obligation on residents and chooses to stimulate it through policy.

230073.explanation: The VVD wants a lively and vibrant city center. Events such as the Summer Festivals can take place in the center and contribute to atmosphere, economy, and tourism.

230074.explanation: The VVD focuses on waste reduction, reuse, and personal responsibility. Monthly free bulky waste collection does not exist: the cost is borne by all residents through waste charges. Residents can dispose of bulky waste themselves; tailored solutions are possible for those who cannot.

230075.explanation: The VVD is open to solar panels on monuments, provided they suit the historical character and do not damage the street image.

230076.explanation: The VVD is cautious about wind energy in Gorinchem due to limited space and the historic landscape. Therefore, it wants to look more broadly at alternative sustainable and more appropriate energy sources.

230077.explanation: Every child must have a good start at school. If that fails at home, support is needed. But a general municipal breakfast for all children is not necessary.

230078.explanation: The VVD values heritage. Sometimes sale is needed to give buildings a better future or owner or to free up resources, always after careful consideration.

230079.explanation: The VVD wants to assess per location what fits best. Artificial turf can be sustainable, but making it mandatory does not exclude tailored solutions and natural grass.

230080.explanation: The VVD sees culture as a valuable public service. A culture house with library and theater can promote efficient space use and cooperation. The municipality facilitates this with manageable costs and demonstrable added value.

230081.explanation: The VVD does not want fixed percentage obligations. Public art is valuable but should come from quality and support, not automatic norms.

230082.explanation: Although this is primarily the responsibility of parents and school, it should be possible to obtain necessary support for certain target groups via, for example, Foundation Leergeld.

230084.explanation: Currently, Gorinchem prohibits sex work in private homes. The VVD wants to maintain this, among other reasons to prevent exploitation.

230085.explanation: The VVD enforces a zero-tolerance policy against illegal dumping of waste. Camera surveillance at hotspots helps tackle offenders and ensures a cleaner, more pleasant living environment.

230086.explanation: Camera surveillance contributes to safety and prevention in the entertainment area, focusing on known problem locations and respecting privacy.

230089.explanation: VVD Gorinchem opposes fixing housing construction to one location. Housing requires tailored solutions and careful consideration, not a predetermined site.

230090.explanation: The VVD does not choose one type of housing in advance. What can be built depends on location, livability, and capacity.

230091.explanation: The VVD supports converting vacant offices into homes, provided sufficient commercial space remains. The municipality can encourage this by simplifying procedures and reducing costs; financial support can be limited and targeted.

230093.explanation: VVD Gorinchem rejects fixed quotas for housing categories. Maximum percentages for expensive rental and owner-occupied homes limit flexibility, hinder movement, and discourage investments. The VVD chooses a varied housing mix with room for middle and higher incomes.

230095.explanation: The VVD sees permanent housing as a solution for young people. Temporary homes in Gorinchem have also proven costly and undesirable compared to permanent residency.

230096.explanation: The VVD believes it is important that residents with low incomes can participate in sports, culture, and other activities. The VVD supports this through appropriate policies and not by introducing a Gorcumpas, which has so far received a negative advisory opinion.

230097.explanation: The VVD thinks it is important that people who can work also participate in society. Meaningful social work helps build routine and work experience and increases the chance of paid employment, with room for tailored solutions if working is temporarily not possible.

230100.explanation: There is already a shortage of parking spaces in the city center. Further reduction harms accessibility for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

230102.explanation: The VVD wants to promote electric driving. More charging stations make sustainable mobility more accessible and contribute to a future-proof transportation system for residents and visitors.

230103.explanation: In case of prolonged closure of the Merwedebrug, the VVD wants to deploy a temporary car ferry. This is important for accessibility, daily traffic, and economic activities in Gorinchem.

230104.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount, especially around schools and busy routes. The VVD opts for tailored solutions: where needed, a 30 km/h speed limit can contribute to safety without unnecessarily restricting traffic flow.

230105.explanation: The VVD sees widening the A15 as crucial for accessibility and economic growth. The municipality should actively lobby the national government to realize this project.

230106.explanation: The VVD wants a well-accessible city center. If expanding the Kweeklust parking garage is necessary to support accessibility and the economy, the municipality can invest in it. This must fit within financial capacity and go hand in hand with parking at the city outskirts.

230107.explanation: The VVD values traffic safety but is cautious about fixed solutions such as bicycle streets. Each street should be assessed for what works best for safety, accessibility, and flow, rather than applying the same measure everywhere.

230108.explanation: Parking on Sunday mornings is already free in Gorinchem. The VVD Gorinchem believes this should apply for the entire Sunday to encourage visits to the city center by day-trippers and shoppers.

230109.explanation: The VVD sees redevelopment of the Groenmarkt as an option but wants to first encourage the use of existing parking garages, day tickets, and peripheral facilities, involving entrepreneurs well in the process.

230112.explanation: The VVD primarily considers couples therapy a personal responsibility. The municipality focuses on support where public interests are at stake, such as youth care and safety.

230113.explanation: The VVD wants poverty to be no obstacle. Targeted provision through schools and social organizations is most effective; a general free policy is not necessary.

230114.explanation: The VVD wants enough general practitioners in Gorinchem. The municipality can help by improving space, procedures, and cooperation-not by providing structural subsidies.

230115.explanation: The VVD wants young people to have places to meet each other in safe or supervised environments, for example through youth work, instead of uncontrolled hangout spots.

230116.explanation: VVD Gorinchem considers a cultural center with a library and other facilities important. Professional performances must be financially independent; the municipality facilitates the center through good conditions but does not invest in large-scale theater productions.

230054.explanation: CU-SGP opposes an additional layer whose representation is unclear. Council members must know residents and be known themselves. Residents must be involved in time and able to participate, especially if it concerns their neighborhood.

230055.explanation: We oppose imposed structures at the neighborhood level. Where positive bottom-up initiatives arise from residents at neighborhood, district, or street level, the municipality can support this with a targeted budget. We also support residents who want to be ambassadors for their neighborhood.

230056.explanation: The municipality of Gorinchem currently uses the organization of the national ombudsman. We support an ombudsman at local or regional level who knows the situation in our municipality, is known, and is easily approachable when residents encounter problems with agencies.

230059.explanation: With the remark that the property tax (OZB) on the owners' share is increased enough to compensate for the elimination of OZB on the users' share. The advantage is that it provides more incentive for owners to reduce vacancy.

230060.explanation: This tourist tax was introduced recently (mid-2024). The rate is well aligned with similar municipalities. Besides an annual adjustment, an increase is not considered by CU-SGP. We do not want to price our city out of the market!

230061.explanation: Significant investments have already been made in city marketing in recent years, having a positive effect on the number of tourists. Within the existing budget, there is room to vary the approach.

230062.explanation: Gorinchem has a large debt that will increase further in the coming years. The debt partly arose from purchasing land for the Groote Haar industrial area. The debt level is high but not problematic. CU-SGP would like to see the debt decline over the longer term to the national average.

230063.explanation: The number of cruise ship visits has risen enormously. Our city is popular for day trips. Many international visitors spend money and contribute to a lively fortress city. Points of attention: preserving free views of the river; our fortress city does not need to become like Giethoorn or Kinderdijk.

230064.explanation: Entrepreneurs bear their own business risks. Vacancy must be addressed by retailer associations, property owners, and the municipality together. Sometimes repurposing or demolition may be necessary to prevent deterioration.

230065.explanation: Addictions such as gambling destroy families, relationships, and society. This leads to many problems like depression, unemployment, and homelessness. Besides personal tragedies, this causes high social costs.

230067.explanation: Gorinchem must meet the requirement. This means we do not want to be lenient here. Gorinchem has a good record in reception and wants other municipalities to also take their responsibilities.

230068.explanation: Among the asylum seekers to be accommodated, unaccompanied minor asylum seekers can also be housed in small-scale settings. With the presence of the International Bridging Class (ISK), we can provide schooling and language lessons. Good cooperation with COA and communication with local residents is crucial!

230069.explanation: There is an excellent structure for providing language lessons through, among others, ROC Da Vinci and the language point Bibliotheek aan Zet. Avres and regional employers are active in offering learning trajectories and volunteer work. Refugees themselves also have a responsibility to integrate as much as possible.

230071.explanation: Gorinchem has several dog exercise areas spread across the city. We have a city with limited land area, where residents also want to relax and exercise. Expanding dog exercise areas is therefore in tension with this. Many people do not feel safe with unleashed dogs.

230072.explanation: CU-SGP is a strong proponent of greening. Both from a biodiversity perspective and for climate adaptation. Greenery provides cooling and drainage of rainwater through the soil instead of via the sewer. The importance of climate adaptation is so great that regulation is justifiable.

230073.explanation: The summer festivals belong to the city and the center for many residents. The program must be appropriate to safeguard livability and safety. This is currently really under pressure. It can be considered to partially move the program to the event location Buiten de Waterpoort.

230074.explanation: This really undermines the principle that 'the polluter pays.' That this can be done 'without costs for residents' is an illusion: this means we all pay together. Waardlanden already offers the possibility to collect bulky waste for a fee. It can also be delivered free within limits.

230075.explanation: Monuments must not be disfigured by solar panels at visible locations, even though there are now more attractive variants. However, if these monuments have opportunities out of sight, this is a good sustainability option.

230076.explanation: Decisions have already been made about 2 to 3 large wind turbines, in addition to 2 medium-sized turbines. All of these will be in Gorinchem North. CU-SGP opposes more wind turbines given our limited territory, effects on local residents, and the landscape's disfigurement.

230077.explanation: Children should have breakfast at home as a good start to the day. If money is the problem, parents should be consulted on how to handle this (extra support, other financial choices?). The responsibility for a good start to the day lies with the parents!

230078.explanation: It is not correct to state that the municipality is always the best caretaker for monuments. However, conditions must/can be set upon sale. For buildings with a public function, such as the old town hall, it is reasonable that the municipality remains the owner.

230079.explanation: One artificial turf field per club is enough to allow a number of activities to continue during bad weather. The production, installation, maintenance, and disposal of artificial turf and rubber granulate negatively affect soil life, nature, and the environment.

230080.explanation: Combining the library and Culture House (note: not a theater like the former Nieuwe Doelen) offers many opportunities. A House for and by the city. The housing of the library is secured for the coming decades. The building De Nieuwe Doelen has a suitable new purpose again.

230081.explanation: It is customized on which locations artworks are placed and what budget is involved. A fixed percentage and linking it to specific projects is experienced by CU-SGP as too rigid and limiting.

230082.explanation: This primarily belongs to the responsibility of schools and parents/caregivers to give this substance. CU-SGP supports additional initiatives, e.g., in neighborhood centers focused on additional homework support for vulnerable students.

230084.explanation: CU-SGP is by definition against sex work. Sex is not work and cannot be given a price. Sexuality comes into its own in a stable relationship/marriage and is therefore priceless. Sex work in residential homes causes nuisance and insecurity. Too often, sex work is the result of coercion.

230085.explanation: Targeted and temporary use of camera surveillance on pollution 'hotspots' can help as an extra measure.

230086.explanation: Targeted and temporary use of camera surveillance on 'hotspots' of nuisance, violence, and vandalism can help as an extra measure.

230089.explanation: CU-SGP believes that many projects are still planned in the built-up area for the next 10-15 years. Think of Arkelsedijk, Schelluinsestraat, Linge South, Gildenwijk. It is currently too early to choose building in this piece of 'green heart,' if that is even allowed.

230090.explanation: Due to the limited area of our city, there must be an emphasis on contemporary stacked construction. A large part of the planned projects will consist of this. Because residents move through here, family homes are vacated. Focus on senior throughput is also necessary.

230091.explanation: Regarding outdated office buildings, this is an excellent choice. However, good office buildings are also necessary to maintain employment levels in our city.

230093.explanation: In line with national policy and the local housing vision, a distribution has been established between percentages of social rent/affordable/expensive housing. This aligns with the CU-SGP priority 'A home that suits you.'

230095.explanation: CU-SGP favors structural solutions. An exploration has shown that flexible housing is a relatively expensive option. At some point, the homes must also be removed again, which presents another challenge.

230096.explanation: Basically, a nice idea to encourage participation of people with low incomes. However, it entirely depends on the chosen implementation. An earlier exploration showed high implementation costs for a relatively small municipality like Gorinchem.

230097.explanation: As a norm, this is a fine starting point. Doing socially meaningful work provides purpose, connects people, and helps them develop. The human scale is required when responding to specific personal circumstances.

230100.explanation: Parking in the fortress should be aimed at local residents and people with mobility impairments. We do want a partial shift of parking capacity from Groenmarkt to parking spaces on the edge of the fortress.

230102.explanation: Yes: charging plazas with a cluster of several charging points in neighborhoods. No: gradually taking over more existing parking spaces in the streets as charging spots.

230103.explanation: The College must keep maximum pressure on Rijkswaterstaat to provide a good alternative when the bridge is not accessible. If financing by the municipality is already under discussion, then only for a ferry connection for local residents (thus no through traffic).

230104.explanation: A fine starting point aligned with the CU-SGP priority 'A cycling Gorinchem!'. This benefits safety as long as communication, signage, and, if necessary, road layout adjustments correspond.

230105.explanation: The A15 between Sliedrecht and the Gorinchem junction is extremely busy, resulting in traffic jams and accidents. Widening up to the junction will help smoother traffic flow and reduce cut-through traffic through the city.

230106.explanation: Only when combined with greening the Groenmarkt, reducing parking capacity in the heart of the center. Expansion of Kweeklust would then truly compensate for parking spaces lost elsewhere. However, realization will be (technically) complex!

230107.explanation: A good analysis of where this is truly feasible and sensible is an important condition. Where we do this, we must do it well. Connects to the CU-SGP priority 'A cycling Gorinchem!'

230108.explanation: CU-SGP supports a day without or with limited economic activity. It is a biblical principle to slow down one day per week and provide space for faith, purpose, and 'encounter.'

230109.explanation: At least largely. This can only be done by compensating these locations elsewhere, e.g., in Kweeklust. CU-SGP strongly favors restoring Groenmarkt’s dignity and making it a pleasant and cooling area with greenery and water.

230112.explanation: CU-SGP favors that relationship therapy and/or programs like marriage courses are widely known and accessible. Families are a cornerstone of society. But those involved must contribute themselves according to their capacity! This point corresponds with the CU-SGP priority 'Stable families.'

230113.explanation: And toothbrushes, razors, bicycle repair kits, bike lights, detergent... It is, of course, good that private initiatives provide such hygiene products for people with minimum incomes.

230114.explanation: As a municipality, it must be considered how to support good coverage of general practitioners across neighborhoods. Simply moving money is seen by CU-SGP as too limited an approach to a complex issue.

230115.explanation: There are multiple places throughout the city suitable for youths to 'hang out.' CU-SGP has little confidence that created places fit well with the changing wishes of youths. With the already planned skatepark, there will be a hangout place anyway.

230116.explanation: The Culture House should focus on local and regional amateur performances. Occasionally a professional performance is fine, but then coordinated with/under responsibility of, e.g., a theater like Peeriscoop. No (professional) program subsidy for the Culture House!

230054.explanation: The municipal council is the elected representative body. A citizens’ assembly is an additional element, comparable to the current digital panel, in which residents are also randomly selected. This proves to be a good addition to decision-making. Citizens’ assembly and digital panel can coexist.

230055.explanation: Gorinchem currently has no neighborhood councils. There was no longer interest in former sounding boards. If neighborhood councils are established, it is good if they have a certain budget for their neighborhood. It promotes involvement if councils can actually do something.

230056.explanation: The municipality’s service scores well in polls. However, some residents or entrepreneurs get stuck in the municipal bureaucracy. Help from an independent ombudsman can be a solution. The ombudsman must not be employed by the municipality.

230059.explanation: Property tax (OZB) applies to real estate. A charge can be made once per property; for the owner or the tenant, not both. For residences, the corporation includes the OZB in the rent. Commercial property owners can also do this.

230060.explanation: Tourists and day-trippers make use of the city's facilities, paid for by residents. A contribution comparable to neighboring municipalities is currently requested, but Gorinchem has so much more to offer. A small increase would be reasonable.

230061.explanation: Gorinchem has been designated the 'most beautiful fortress city.' The fortifications are UNESCO World Heritage. It is good to continually raise awareness, as tourism not only brings life to the city but also income. It helps maintain the facilities.

230062.explanation: Gorinchem annually pays off part of its debts. There is an investment ceiling within which large investments, such as school buildings and roads, can certainly be made. Those investments are urgently needed.

230063.explanation: The first mooring is so well visited that a second may be needed. Day tourists often visit the city briefly but usually return for a weekend or a week. Cruise ships are very welcome.

230064.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to support themselves. Otherwise, the municipality, with taxpayers' money, would pay for the vacancy of property owners. That is the reverse. Property owners must ensure their buildings are rentable. Then there is no vacancy.

230065.explanation: That would be patronizing. The municipality earns money from advertisements. Taste is subjective. Some find casinos bad, others candy and soft drinks. The consumer decides.

230067.explanation: Gorinchem has been hosting asylum seekers for decades and meets the requirements of the Distribution Act with the new AZC at Buys Ballotstraat. That is sufficient. Additionally, Gorinchem hosts more than 300 Ukrainians and many labor migrants. The city is reaching its limits.

230068.explanation: Gorinchem has been hosting asylum seekers for decades and the new AZC on Buys Ballotstraat meets the requirements of the Distribution Act. That is sufficient. Additionally, Gorinchem is hosting over 300 Ukrainians. The city is reaching the limits of its capacity. Neighboring municipalities will be responsible for unaccompanied minor asylum seekers.

230069.explanation: Language lessons and facilities for refugees are provided. These are paid for not by the municipality, but by the COA (Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers).

230071.explanation: Dogs should be able to run freely. Every neighborhood should have such a facility within walking distance, preferably equipped with dog poop bags to keep the environment clean.

230072.explanation: Obligations are never pleasant, but they would be desirable. Gorcum Actief believes more in incentives, such as education, rather than obligations. With new construction, discount coupons for trees and plants could be a good incentive.

230073.explanation: The Summer Festivals have taken place in the city center for ages. The volunteer organization deserves all respect and support. Thousands of young people enjoy this one week of festivities annually. Scaling down somewhat and having more stages would make it more enjoyable. There is no alternative location for these festivals.

230074.explanation: Only if the number of added waste items on the street decreases as a result. Driving extra rounds monthly incurs costs, which residents pay through waste collection fees. Waste is not free. But currently, many residents dump waste on the street. That also costs money.

230075.explanation: That is possible within the Protected Cityscape (the entire city center) under certain conditions.

230076.explanation: Two large wind turbines will be installed north of Gorinchem as part of the energy transition at the Groote Haar industrial estate. Other previously mentioned locations such as Gorinchem-East, Lingewijk and Avelingen are not suitable. Gorinchem North is the only option.

230077.explanation: Breakfast at school might be a good idea, as it improves learning performance. All children could benefit from it. It should then be arranged at the national level with corresponding funding, or parents could contribute themselves. Currently, children simply eat at home.

230078.explanation: It concerns all historic buildings, including those outside the fortress. The municipality currently has Heritage Policy and must take good care of its heritage, so in principle, not sell it. Exceptions can be made under strict conditions.

230079.explanation: That depends entirely on how often a field will be used. With heavy use, artificial turf is the better choice.

230080.explanation: The realization of the Cultural Center is an important investment for the city. In that building, there is room for the library, associations, cultural activities, etc. The large hall should also preferably be well utilized. Investing in culture is investing in your city and its residents.

230081.explanation: This resembles the former 1% art scheme. Gorinchem has a lot of public art thanks to various symposiums. Maintaining and cherishing all that art is a major task. It would be better to save resources for a new good symposium.

230082.explanation: Many children and young people could use a helping hand. Homework guidance can help, either at school or another location. The municipality can contribute to this.

230084.explanation: The municipality does not govern activities that take place behind people's front doors at home.

230085.explanation: Dumping waste is such a big problem that temporary camera surveillance at certain locations is desirable. Currently, the community bears the cost of this misconduct.

230086.explanation: There is sufficient camera surveillance in the nightlife area.

230089.explanation: The Groote Haar industrial estate will be located in Gorinchem-North. It is highly questionable whether it is desirable to also develop a residential neighborhood there. There is too little space. Gorinchem is reaching its limits. Perhaps temporary housing for labor migrants. Otherwise, expansion towards Arkel.

230090.explanation: There is a need for both. For future new construction on Schelluinsestraat, one or more tower blocks could be built. In and around the city center and neighborhoods, the preference is for lower buildings and affordable family homes.

230091.explanation: Property owners have their own interest in feasible plans. It is not the municipality's role to finance them with public funds. In certain cases, national subsidies can be obtained as incentives.

230093.explanation: The ratio of one-third owner-occupied, one-third affordable, and one-third social housing is fine. Developers usually earn the most on expensive homes. Therefore, the municipality must oversee good proportions.

230095.explanation: The housing shortage among young people is the highest. Especially if they are still single, there is no chance on the housing market. Maybe flexible housing can help them get started. Gorcum Actief wants to investigate whether a cruise ship for starting singles could serve as a springboard onto the housing market.

230096.explanation: The discount pass is for all residents interested, not only for low-income households. The group of 'working poor' is growing, but other residents also contribute to services through local taxes. Prices for the swimming pool and theater are rising. So, the discount pass is for all Gorcumers.

230097.explanation: Sitting 'behind the geraniums' has never made anyone better. It helps society if a reciprocal service is delivered, but above all, it is good and healthy for the volunteer to make a meaningful contribution.

230100.explanation: Not yet. A car-reduced city center is more attractive, but currently there are insufficient alternatives in garages or outside the fortress.

230102.explanation: The number of electric cars will increase, and so will the demand for charging points. Places can be reserved in consultation with the neighborhood. In some neighborhoods, a charging plaza could be a solution.

230103.explanation: The Riveer ferry service exists thanks to the participating municipalities. When work is done on the A27 and the Merwede bridge, the ferry can be an alternative for residents and visitors. The municipality contributes financially; Rijkswaterstaat (the national water management agency) should also contribute.

230104.explanation: Gorinchem is not a safe cycling city, but hundreds of students cycle daily. Gorcum Actief wants safe cycling routes for residents and students. Motorists must adjust their speed. On some routes, 'cars are guests' can be introduced.

230105.explanation: That is pointless, because the A15 has already been newly paved and will not be further widened. That is the minister's decision. Gorinchem, in cooperation with Smart Delta (Drechtsteden), can better lobby for alternatives such as double tracks on the Merwede-Linge line and for public transport hubs near the highways.

230106.explanation: The municipality must invest in parking spaces. Either the expansion of Kweeklust or elsewhere outside the fortress. The latter is preferred. Fewer cars in the city center is more attractive. Gorcum Actief wants more greenery and, for example, picnic tables in the Groenmarkt.

230107.explanation: In the city, there is often little space for both cars and other uses simultaneously. Then 'cars are guests' is a good option or a car ban. Other cities like Utrecht are already successful with this.

230108.explanation: On Sundays, the city is usually less busy. How wonderful if one day a week there is no hassle with paying. And a stimulus for culture, hospitality, and business.

230109.explanation: The Groenmarkt could become much greener again if alternative parking is available elsewhere. Gorcum Actief sees such an approach as gradual. First, freeing part for example for greenery and picnic places or benches where visitors can sit. Currently, there is only hospitality and parking. A pity.

230112.explanation: First of all, parents must want to work on their relationship if things are not good at home. That is their choice and responsibility. The municipality can at most contribute.

230113.explanation: Most residents can buy their preferred sanitary pads themselves. For low-income households, there are already arrangements for free supplies. At secondary schools and some locations in the city, free sanitary pads are already provided.

230114.explanation: The municipality must ensure that there is a place in every neighborhood where general practitioners can establish themselves. So, reserve space or locations for this. The municipality can help with facilitation, not financing. Due to aging, the demand for care will increase, and the number of general practitioners will decrease.

230115.explanation: It is actually good if young people want to meet each other. Hangout spots, small soccer fields, etc. Gorcum Actief wants a cozy youth center in the city center where they can also engage with music and bands.

230116.explanation: Absolutely! The municipality invests a lot in the Cultural Center with the large hall. An operating budget must also be made available. The coordinator of the Cultural Center can organize a number of professional performances, for example for families or specific target groups. That hall is there to be used.

230054.explanation: D66 advocates for more opportunities for residents to have a say. This way, Gorcumers can make their voices heard about decisions that concern them. A citizen's assembly is one of the options we would like to explore.

230055.explanation: Residents know best what happens in their neighborhood. Therefore, D66 supports neighborhood councils with their own neighborhood budget. This way, residents can decide for themselves how they want to make their neighborhood more livable, greener, or more social. This returns say and trust to the city.

230056.explanation: If residents experience problems with the municipality, D66 wants them to have access to an independent ombudsman. This person offers a listening ear and thinks along about solutions.

230059.explanation: D66 does not want to simply give up the possibilities the law offers for levying these municipal taxes. These revenues contribute to covering the municipal budget and thus the welfare of our residents.

230060.explanation: D66 chooses a modest tourist tax as is currently the case. There is no reason to raise it now.

230061.explanation: D66 wants the municipality and entrepreneurs to collaborate on sustainable tourism with attention to the livability of the city center. However, attracting tourists is not a task for the municipality. We do ensure maintenance of public spaces, preservation of heritage, and a thriving cultural sector.

230062.explanation: D66 stands for solid and responsible municipal finances. We make room to continue investing without high debts for future generations. There is currently no reason to reduce debts at the expense of investments.

230063.explanation: D66 cherishes the current spacious berth for passenger ships, alongside the berths for commercial shipping and for ferry services. The interests of the latter two are significant for Gorinchem and deserve the full space currently allocated. We want to keep it that way.

230064.explanation: D66 supports subsidies for pop-up shops to combat temporary vacancies in shopping areas. D66 wants an investigation into the possibilities to subsidize the start of specialty shops and neighborhood facilities in all district and neighborhood shopping streets where necessary.

230065.explanation: D66 is very cautious about banning legally permitted advertisements in public spaces.

230067.explanation: D66 stands for humane and balanced refugee reception. We take our responsibility and are also willing to temporarily do more than the law requires, for example if inhumane situations occur in Ter Apel.

230068.explanation: D66 takes responsibility for refugee reception. This also applies to the reception of 24 unaccompanied minor foreigners assigned to Gorinchem under the Distribution Act. We focus, for example, on (youth) care, language lessons, and sports to help integration.

230069.explanation: D66 wants to offer refugees in Gorinchem language lessons and work experience places from the very start. For this, the municipality will need to allocate extra resources to provide this with the help of many volunteers.

230071.explanation: D66 favors designating dog exercise areas in the large parks and green spaces in the city. Dog waste must always be cleaned up there as well. We live in a clean city. In other places, dogs must remain on a leash.

230072.explanation: Our climate is changing. Heat stress and extreme rainfall are occurring more frequently. More greenery and space for rainwater retention are essential. Therefore, D66 wants the municipality to encourage residents to voluntarily green their gardens and yards. D66 does not want to make this mandatory but wants to strongly encourage it.

230073.explanation: D66 wants future generations to continue enjoying events like the Summer Festivals in the city center. We pay attention to livability but also explore how we can support events that are at risk of disappearing.

230074.explanation: Everyone wants to live in a clean city. That is why D66 tackles litter and illegal dumping by making bulky waste disposal easier. D66 wants bulky waste to be collected free of charge and monthly at home.

230075.explanation: Gorinchem has beautiful monumental buildings and protected cityscapes. D66 wants to make their sustainability improvements easier, for example with solar panels or green roofs. At the same time, we want to preserve heritage, which requires tailored solutions.

230076.explanation: D66 does not want wind turbines to block housing construction. We therefore question whether there is room in Gorinchem for large (mega) windmills and draw attention to small-scale alternatives.

230077.explanation: D66 wants all primary schools to join the Rich School Day program. This way, children encounter sports, nature, culture, music, healthy school breakfasts and/or lunches, and homework support at school and during after-school care.

230078.explanation: Heritage in our municipality is important for Gorinchem's identity and our community feeling. D66 wants to invest in strengthening, preservation, and visibility of this. Municipal ownership is not always necessary for this. However, we collaborate with organizations such as the Gorinchem City Restoration Foundation.

230079.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should be a good and reliable partner for all sports clubs regarding rental of sports fields. Depending on national standards for fields, we must continually assess whether investment in an artificial turf field is necessary and desirable.

230080.explanation: D66 supports the new Culture House. This should strengthen the existing cultural offer with a large stage for local talent, school performances, amateur associations, and much more. It will also house the library, which needs a new location.

230081.explanation: D66 wants to invest in the livability and attractiveness of our living environment. We therefore support the 1% regulation.

230082.explanation: D66 wants all primary schools to join the Rich School Day program. This way, children encounter sports, nature, culture, music, healthy school breakfasts and/or lunches, and homework support at school and during after-school care.

230084.explanation: D66 has no objection to working from home, even when it involves sex work. This also prevents sex workers from ending up illegally.

230085.explanation: D66 opposes camera surveillance for waste dumping because this only shifts the problem elsewhere.

230086.explanation: D66 focuses on preventive and proven effective measures to tackle nuisance and crime. There is no reason to place cameras in the entertainment district; therefore, this is currently undesirable.

230089.explanation: D66 wants a new, sustainable residential area in Gorinchem-North with stacked housing, preservation of green and water. The neighborhood offers space for nature, social interaction, and facilities, with room on the ground floor for businesses and studios.

230090.explanation: D66 chooses sustainable, stacked houses and smart densification in existing neighborhoods. By adding extra floors and better use of ground floors, space is saved while preserving greenery and paying attention to parking and traffic.

230091.explanation: D66 wants to transform vacant, often somewhat outdated office buildings into homes, such as those along Stationsweg.

230093.explanation: D66 focuses on variety in the housing supply in our city. There must be a place for everyone in every neighborhood. To achieve this balance, we do not choose to set a maximum number of more expensive homes. It is tailored per neighborhood.

230095.explanation: Having your own place when you outgrow the parental home or during your further studies is essential for young adults. We want young people (students and other groups) to more easily share housing (student houses, shared apartments).

230096.explanation: A municipal discount card is expensive to produce, manage, and secure. We would rather spend that money on the goal: supporting people with lower incomes. We prefer to do this as targeted as possible.

230097.explanation: D66 sees social assistance as an essential safety net for existential security. We make minimum schemes more accessible and support vulnerable residents with poverty, debts, and return to work. This should not involve additional coercion from the municipality beyond what the national government already provides.

230100.explanation: D66 wants to expand the Kweeklust parking garage and better utilize the parking garage at Kazerneplein. This allows cars to disappear more from the street scene while keeping sufficient parking spaces available in the city center.

230102.explanation: D66 invests in a comprehensive network of charging stations everywhere in the city. We encourage charging options at parking garages, offices, shopping centers, and private parking spots. This way, we avoid having to sacrifice public parking spaces.

230103.explanation: Rijkswaterstaat will have to make temporary arrangements in close consultation with all parties involved. D66 has full confidence in this.

230104.explanation: D66's starting point is a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h within built-up areas. This gives road users more time to anticipate each other and prevents (fatal) accidents. Especially where pedestrians and cyclists encounter cars, we lower the maximum speed.

230105.explanation: D66 supports rapid widening of the entire A15. This benefits safety and traffic flow on the heavily used road. This is also in Gorinchem's interest. It will partially reduce shortcut traffic through the city.

230106.explanation: D66 still wants to realize expansion of the Kweeklust parking garage and better use the Kazerneplein parking garage. This relieves parking pressure in the city center.

230107.explanation: Especially for short distances, D66 wants to give ample space to pedestrians and cyclists. We therefore invest in wide sidewalks and cycle paths, good bicycle connections, priority arrangements at intersections, and safe-to-use routes in all weather conditions and seasons.

230108.explanation: In Gorinchem, you have to pay to park in the city center. D66 sees no reason why the municipality would not want to receive this income on Sundays.

230109.explanation: D66 wants to still expand the Kweeklust parking garage and better utilize the Kazerneplein parking garage. This will relieve parking pressure and make it possible in the long term to responsibly make the Groenmarkt car-reduced or car-free.

230112.explanation: D66 believes that relationship therapy should be provided in consultation with the general practitioner team and reimbursed by the health insurer. Normally, the municipality does not have a role in this.

230113.explanation: Within the framework of livelihood security and poverty reduction, D66 believes the municipality should distribute free tampons at various locations.

230114.explanation: D66 wants a general practitioner within walking distance for all residents of Gorinchem. We support general practitioners to ensure this, including financially.

230115.explanation: D66 invests in the well-being of young people in our city. This also includes more hangout spots.

230116.explanation: D66 holds the Culture House close to heart. We opt for a large stage for local talent and amateur associations.

230054.explanation: The CDA believes it is the municipal council's task to know what is happening, to go to the neighborhood and speak with residents. Through a citizen's assembly, Gorcumers are unwillingly asked to do the council's work.

230055.explanation: Residents know better than anyone what is needed in their neighborhood. The municipality should base its actions on trust; the CDA trusts that neighborhood councils will use their funds wisely and responsibly. This can increase involvement of residents in their neighborhoods.

230056.explanation: Residents can turn to the national ombudsman for conflicts, including local issues. This should be clearly stated on the municipality's website. We are open to a regional ombudsman, but an ombudsman solely for Gorinchem is not appropriate and leads to unnecessarily high costs.

230059.explanation: Such a measure is not effective; owners will immediately pass this cost on to tenants, merely cycling money around.

230060.explanation: It is important that everyone contributes to the facilities in our city, including the many tourists who come here. The CDA wants to keep the burden on residents as low as possible with this.

230061.explanation: Tourism is an important source of income for retailers and the municipality. We must also seek ways to increase tourist spending in Gorinchem.

230062.explanation: Financially challenging years lie ahead for all municipalities, including Gorinchem, due to reduced contributions from the national government. The CDA wants to pass Gorinchem on well to future generations and therefore not live on credit. We maintain the investment ceiling.

230063.explanation: Currently, multiple cruise ships can dock simultaneously in Gorinchem. The CDA believes it is unnecessary to spend more money on this; we prefer to invest in housing construction, safe roads and bike paths, and vulnerable groups.

230064.explanation: The CDA believes this is not a municipal task but that of collaborating entrepreneurs and property owners. Moreover, this also disrupts the level playing field. This money could be better spent on attractive and safe shopping areas.

230065.explanation: More and more people, especially young people, get into trouble because of gambling addiction and debts. The CDA is therefore against promoting gambling. We align with the national policy to further reduce this kind of advertising in public spaces.

230067.explanation: Gorinchem has always taken responsibility for asylum seeker reception. The CDA finds it important to continue doing so and expects the same from surrounding municipalities. This prevents further pressure on our facilities (and thus good reception).

230068.explanation: The CDA is critical regarding asylum inflow in the Netherlands, but for those who truly need our help, especially minors, we must always be ready. We have always done this and will continue to do so; we take our responsibility.

230069.explanation: Many Gorcumers commit themselves to the well-being and integration of refugees. The CDA values this; we must continue (or even increase) investment where necessary. Currently, this cannot always be well spent. So more investment if needed and useful, but we guard efficiency!

230071.explanation: There are already several off-leash dog areas in our city, including parts of the city walls and the Gijsbert van Andelpark. At the same time, we see increasing nuisance from dog feces. We would rather invest in providing poop bags at these locations.

230072.explanation: The CDA supports more greenery. But we want to impose as few obligations as possible on residents, and legally this is not possible either. However, the municipality can promote more greenery by providing seeds and plants, sharing knowledge, and supporting neighborhood initiatives.

230073.explanation: The CDA is a fan of the Summer Festivals! But we have doubts about the current form's sustainability. Celebrations are for everyone, so we want to discuss with the organizers, residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors how this can remain a celebration for all Gorcumers, as intended!

230074.explanation: The CDA stands for the principle: the polluter pays. We therefore want everyone to pay for their own waste. However, we halve the fee for waste collected at home.

230075.explanation: Solar panels are an important part of the energy transition for the CDA, including in the city center. There are monuments where solar panels can make a very good contribution, but custom work is needed. Solar panels are not suitable or safe on every monument.

230076.explanation: The CDA wants to work towards a green and sustainable future for our (grand)children. This includes wind turbines to promote clean air and dry feet. We also see opportunities for the municipality to earn from this.

230077.explanation: Breakfast is the most important meal of the day, especially for growing youth. Organizing a joint breakfast at schools can help. However, customization is needed; not every school requires this, and national initiatives can also be joined.

230078.explanation: As Gorcumers, we are very proud of our beautiful fortified city. For the CDA, this also means we must cherish our material heritage and handle it carefully. As owners, you have the most influence on this.
